dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HRES 1408 IH: Recognizing the State of Michigan’s contributions to the United States on the occasion of the 250th anniversary of the Union.
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Moolenaar (for himself, Mrs. McClain, Ms. McDonald Rivet, Mr. Walberg, Mr. Barrett, Mr. Huizenga, Mrs. Dingell, Ms. Stevens, Ms. Tlaib, Mr. Thanedar, Ms. Scholten, Mr. Bergman, and Mr. James) submitted the following resolution; which was referred to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform

form.official-title: Recognizing the State of Michigan’s contributions to the United States on the occasion of the 250th anniversary of the Union.

whereas.text: Whereas, on January 26, 1837, Michigan became the 26th State of the United States;
Whereas Michigan and its residents embody the ideals of the Founding and have significantly contributed to the success and advancement of the United States;
Whereas July 4, 2026, is the 250th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence; and

Whereas the 250th anniversary provides an opportunity to reflect on the Founding of the United States and the lasting impact that Michiganders have made on the United States since its entrance into the Union: Now, therefore, be it

paragraph.text: recognizes the 250th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence;
encourages all United States citizens to reflect on the ideals of the Founders and their fight for independence; and
celebrates the enduring impact that Michiganders have made on the success of the United States.
